plant.description: Unique History: Classified under the 'Deepaniya' (appetizer) and 'Shothahara' (anti-inflammatory) groups in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita. The name 'Hingu' signifies its ability to 'protect the gut'. It is spiritually associated with 'Sattva' (purity and clarity). Mentioned in the Vedas and Puranas for its unmatched 'Shulaprashamana' properties. Physical Characteristics: A perennial, giant, herbaceous plant growing up to 2 meters in height, with a thick, fleshy, aromatic taproot that produces the oleo-gum-resin. The leaves are large, pinnately divided, and green. The flowers are small, yellowish-green, in large terminal umbels. The resin is collected from the root and stem by making incisions. It is found in the arid regions of Afghanistan, Iran, and high-altitude Western Himalayas. Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Essential Oil (Organic Sulfides), Resin (Asaresinotannol), and Gum. The presence of organic sulfides is crucial for its anti-spasmodic, anti-carcinogenic, and anti-inflammatory action on the immune system. Strict Standardizations: The resin is standardized in API based on its yellowish-brown or greyish-white color and characteristic strong-pungent-garlic-like odor. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<15%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>50%), and water-soluble extractive (>50%). HPLC is used to monitor 10% to 15% volatile oil in commercial extracts. Purification Process (Shodhana): The raw resin is traditionally fried in 'Ghee' or 'Taila' until it becomes crisp and loses its nauseating odor, followed by washing with hot water to remove excessive irritant properties before medicinal use.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Powerful Vata and Kapha Hara (pacifies Vata and Kapha doshas). Due to its Katu (pungent) Rasa and Atyanta-Ushna (extremely hot) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Shulaprashamana' (pain-relieving) and 'Anulomana' (downward-moving) therapies, clearing the cold, heavy, and sticky qualities of Kapha while balancing the dry and rough qualities of Vata through its systemic downward-flowing property. It may significantly aggravate Pitta if used in excess.

purified_resin_powder.method: 125 mg to 500 mg of purified resin fried in Ghee and taken with warm water or buttermilk (as a digestive Anupana) before meals.

hingvashtaka_churna.method: Combined with Ginger, Black pepper, Long pepper, Salt, Cumin, and Nigella for systemic metabolism and respiratory health.

hingvadi_vati.method: Classical tablet form used for indigestion and gas; taken (250-500mg) after or during meals for immediate relief.

root_whole_plant_paste.method: Ground purified resin or paste applied externally to the navel (especially for infants) for instant relief from abdominal gas and colic.

hing_taila.method: Purified resin processed in sesame oil; used for local massage and Danta-Dhavana (tooth-strengthening oil technique).

dosage.children: Generally safe. 10 mg to 30 mg of purified resin mixed with ghee and applied to the navel or in honey twice daily for respiratory and digestive protection.
dosage.adults: Powder: 250-500 mg; Tablet: 250-500 mg; Purified resin: 125-500 mg, divided throughout the day before or during meals.
dosage.elderly: Ideal for age-related Vata issues and maintaining digestive health. Dosage: 125mg of purified resin with warm water before meals.

medicinal_uses: Expert Digestive and Gas reliever Specialist: Hingu is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for all conditions of abdominal gas, bloating, and colic. Its Tikshna (sharp) and Atyanta-Ushna (extremely hot) nature helps in instantaneously clearing 'Vata-avarana' (Vata-obstruction) in the gut and providing instant relief from abdominal cramps. WHO-aligned research confirms its antispasmodic and carminative effects.

Intestinal Worm and Parasite Care: Its 'Krimighna' (worm-destroying) property is utilized primarily in external applications (as a paste) and internally (in very small purified doses) to resolve various intestinal parasites and worms that thrive in a high-Kapha environment.

Heart and Cardiac health Support: Acts as a powerful agent for managing cardiovascular health (Hrid-roga). It prevents the blockage of blood vessels and improves circulation by stimulating the 'Agni' (metabolic fire) within the tissues (Dhatus) and clearing systemic toxins (Ama).

Respiratory and Cough support: Its Atyanta-Ushna (heating) potency helps in liquefying and expelling thick mucus from the lungs through its Tikshna-Katu (sharp-pungent) action. It is traditionally used in 'Kasa' (cough) and 'Shwasa' (asthma) when there is excess Kapha accumulation.

Menstrual Support and Pain: Historically known as 'Rajasthapana' (menstruation-restoring) and 'Artava-janana'. It is the most prestigious herb for managing painful menstruation (Dysmenorrhea) and irregular periods by providing systemic downward-flowing energy (Apana-Vata).

Digestive and Liver Detox: Its Tikta (bitter) and Katu (pungent) properties help in liver detoxification and healthy bile secretion. It is traditionally used to treat jaundice and fatty liver by reducing inflammatory load and regenerating hepatocytes.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Resin (approx. 40-60%), Gum (approx. 25%), and Volatile Oil (approx. 10-15%). The oil contains diversas Sulfides (Di-methyl sulfide), Pinene, and various Sesquiterpenoids. The presence of organic sulfides is crucial for its anti-spasmodic and anti-microbial action.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb and spice ONLY when properly purified (Shodhana) and taken in recommended therapeutic dosages without documented toxicity.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Strictly contraindicated in individuals with active high-Pitta (burning sensation, hyperacidity, bleeding disorders) and during pregnancy in large doses. Avoid in extreme skin sensitivity. Toxicity is high in non-purified resin.
possible_side_effects: Overdose or use in high Pitta individuals can cause stomach burning, vomiting, diarrhea, skin rashes, and excessive heartbeat.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-diuretic or anticoagulant medications as it may potentiate their effects. No severe adverse interactions reported in clinical literature.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
